title: DC30 Lab 1: Run HELLO — First Mainframe Program
description: Log in to TSO, learn ISPF navigation, and run your first mainframe program. The HELLO binary uses gets() — a deliberately vulnerable C function. This is the foundation for all buffer overflow labs.

steps.title: Connect and Log In
steps.screen:
VTAM LOGON SCREEN

 Enter your TSO userid and press ENTER.

 Userid   ===> HERC01
 Password ===> (enter CUL8TR)

 TK5 MVS 3.8j — TN3270 on port 3270
 Connect with: x3270 localhost:3270
 Or use the web terminal at http://localhost:8080
steps.expected: You are at the TSO READY prompt after logging in.
steps.hint: On TK5 use HERC01 / CUL8TR. In the original DC30 workshop users were DC00-DC22 with matching passwords.

steps.title: Run the HELLO Program
steps.screen:
READY
 CALL 'HERC01.LOAD(HELLO)'

 Hi, D3FC0N attendee what is your handle?
 _

 Note: Always include the single quotes around the dataset name.
 Before running, clear the screen with: CLS
steps.expected: The HELLO program prints its greeting and waits for your input.
steps.hint: CALL is the TSO command to execute a load module from a PDS. The format is CALL 'HLQ.LOAD(MEMBER)'. The program uses gets() — no bounds checking on the 150-byte buffer.

steps.title: ISPF Primer — Accessing Files
steps.screen:
TSO READY
 ISPF

 ---- ISPF PRIMARY OPTION MENU ----
  0  Settings
  1  View
  2  Edit
  3  Utilities
  4  Foreground
  5  Batch
  6  Command
  7  Dialog Test
  9  IBM Products
  10 SCLM
  11 Workplace

 Option ===>
steps.expected: You are at the ISPF Primary Option Menu.
steps.hint: Type ISPF at the READY prompt to enter ISPF. Use =3.4 to browse datasets, =3.8 to view job output. F3 exits any screen. F2 opens a second window, F9 cycles between windows.

steps.title: Browse Your Datasets
steps.screen:
ISPF OPTION ===> 3.4

  DSLIST - Data Sets Matching HERC01
  Command ===> ___________

  Dataset Name                       Volume
  HERC01.JCLLIB                      PUB001
  HERC01.LOAD                        PUB001
  HERC01.OVERFLOW                    PUB001
  HERC01.DUMP001                     PUB001
steps.expected: You can see your personal datasets: JCLLIB, LOAD, OVERFLOW, DUMP001.
steps.hint: Type =3.4 from anywhere in ISPF to reach the dataset list. Enter your HLQ (HERC01) in the prefix field. Put V next to a dataset to view it, E to edit, S to browse members.

steps.title: Access LAB01 JCL
steps.expected: You can see the JCL for Lab 1 in the ISPF editor.
steps.hint: Navigate: =3.4 → put E next to HERC01.JCLLIB → put E next to LAB01. The STDIN DD points to an overflow file — this is what gets fed to gets() in the HELLO program.

steps.title: Submit and View Output
steps.screen:
EDIT - HERC01.JCLLIB(LAB01)
 Command ===> SUBMIT

 IKJ56250I JOB LAB01(JOB00123) SUBMITTED

 --- View output: =3.8 ---

 SDSF OUTPUT DISPLAY  HERC01  JOB00123
  NP JOBNAME  JobID    Owner    Prty Queue  C Pos  SAff  ASid  Status
     LAB01    JOB00123 HERC01   1    PRINT  A

 MAX COND CODE 0000
steps.expected: Job LAB01 completes with MAX COND CODE 0000.
steps.hint: Type SUBMIT on the command line while in the JCL editor. Then =3.8 to view job output. Put S next to the job to see the sysout. MAX COND CODE 0000 means success.
